skills.skill: Convert between place values
skills.prompt: This topic covers the relationship between place values for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.2(A)). Students should understand that each place value position is 10 times the value of the position to its right and 1/10 the value of the position to its left. Key concepts: the standard algorithm involves multiplying each digit of one factor by each digit of the other, accounting for place value; partial products break the multiplication into simpler steps; area models connect multiplication to geometric representations; estimation helps verify reasonableness. Students should handle regrouping (carrying) accurately. Vocabulary: multiply, factor, product, partial product, regroup, carry, algorithm, place value, area model, digit, estimate. Problems should be appropriate for 9-10 year olds.

skills.skill: Division facts to 12 (2)
skills.prompt: This topic covers division facts for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.4(F)). Students should demonstrate fluency with division facts through 144 / 12. Key concepts: division is the inverse of multiplication; knowing multiplication facts supports division fluency; dividend / divisor = quotient; if a x b = c, then c / a = b and c / b = a; division by zero is undefined. Vocabulary: divide, dividend, divisor, quotient, inverse, fact family, multiplication, equal groups, share, partition, fluency, remainder. Problems should build speed and accuracy appropriate for 9-10 year olds.

skills.skill: Choose numbers with a particular quotient
skills.prompt: This topic covers choosing numbers with a particular result for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.4(F)). Students should understand how to select numbers from a set that produce a specified sum, difference, product, or quotient. Key concepts: use estimation and number sense to narrow down possibilities; test combinations systematically; understanding inverse operations helps (if looking for a sum, think about what addends are needed); this reinforces operation fluency and algebraic thinking. Vocabulary: choose, select, sum, difference, product, quotient, estimate, combination, target, result, test, number sense, operation, reasoning. Problems should be appropriate for 9-10 year olds.

skills.skill: Divide 2-digit numbers by 1-digit numbers: interpret remainders
skills.prompt: This topic covers dividing multi-digit numbers for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.4(H)). Students should understand the standard long division algorithm and alternative division strategies. Key concepts: long division involves dividing, multiplying, subtracting, and bringing down digits; partial quotients is an alternative where students subtract multiples of the divisor; remainders may need to be interpreted in context (ignored, rounded up, or expressed as a fraction/decimal); estimating quotients helps with the first digit placement. Vocabulary: divide, dividend, divisor, quotient, remainder, long division, partial quotient, estimate, algorithm, place value, regroup. Problems should be appropriate for 9-10 year olds.
